name: understand
tagline: Scans a codebase with LLM subagents to build an interactive architecture knowledge-graph, layer map, and guided tour under .ua/.
verdict: Mostly solid: no secret reads, no network exfiltration, and explicit instructions to treat README/manifest content as untrusted, ignoring embedded directives. Concerns for an installer: SKILL.md is 859 lines (well over the ~500 budget); it auto rm -rf's old trash dirs every run, with wording that frames this as dodging hardened-host 'destructive-action gates

highlights.summary: Phase 0 step 3.1 runs `find "$UA_DIR/" ... -mtime +7 -exec rm -rf {} +` on every invocation with no user confirmation. Text (line 139) explicitly frames the mv-then-delayed-delete design as existing '

Same passage as S04: 'Phase 7 cleanup mvs scratch dirs into .trash-<timestamp>/ rather than rm -rf'ing them directly ... so that destructive-action gates on hardened hosts don't trip on just-created p

First-run bootstrap: `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile 2>/dev/null || pnpm install` (line 118) silently falls back to a non-frozen install if the frozen one fails, which can pull different/updated trans
